_instructions: Copy this file to {state_name_lowercase}.json and fill in all fields. Upload to S3 config bucket to activate monitoring for this state.
cdc_geography_name: REPLACE Full State Name (must match CDC NSSP geography field exactly)

REPLACE_MSA_FIPS.name: REPLACE Full MSA Name (e.g., 'Phoenix-Mesa-Chandler')
REPLACE_MSA_FIPS.notes: REPLACE with any relevant notes about this metro's disease behavior

_sentinel_metros_notes: Include 2-4 major metros that serve as sentinel hubs for the state.
Ideal metros: large population, strong healthcare infrastructure, upstream of rural areas.
For states without dominant metros (MT, WY, VT), consider using neighboring state metros.
_subscribing_counties_notes: Add one entry per rural county that wants to receive alerts.
primary_metro_affinity: MSA code of the metro whose signal most predicts this county.
affinity_weights: how much weight to give each metro (must sum to 1.0).
alert_threshold: minimum severity to trigger delivery (LOW, MODERATE, HIGH, CRITICAL).
The system auto-discovers county relationships if you run seed_calibration_data.py.

disease_overrides._comment: Optional per-state overrides. Omit or set to null to use disease config defaults.
